name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is the leading enterprise AI platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI agents to eliminate enterprise friction. ServiceNow serves 8,100+ customers including 85% of the Fortune 500, with Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B growing 22% YoY.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: ServiceNow has fundamentally transformed from an ITSM ticketing system to the enterprise AI platform — expanding TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ [1]. AI revenue target raised 50% to $1.5B for 2026, with CEO McDermott declaring ServiceNow 'the AI control tower for business reinvention' [3]. Q1 2026 subscription revenue grew 22% YoY to $3.67B, beating guidance, while cRPO growth of 25% demonstrates accelerating AI-driven demand [1]. The Armis acquisition and Arc platform for agentic AI governance position ServiceNow to control the autonomous AI execution layer that enterprises require [3].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: ServiceNow's mission to 'make the world of work, work better for people' is specific, validated, and measurable through productivity outcomes [3]. McDermott emphasizes 'there is no artificial intelligence without human intelligence' and frames AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them — creating psychological safety at scale [3]. The company ranks on Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Fortune World's Most Admired Companies, and Glassdoor Best Places to Work simultaneously [3]. McDermott's $3M personal share purchase and contract extension to 2030 demonstrate mission-level conviction [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Q1 2026 revenue of $3.77B grew 22% YoY with subscription revenue guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B for FY2026 (20.5-21% CC growth) [1]. Post-10:1 split, the stock trades at $95 with forward P/E of 18.9x and PEG of 0.88 — exceptionally attractive for a 22% grower with 32% operating margins [4]. ROE of 16.1% is solid, debt-to-equity of 20.7% is conservative, and the $162M business performance upside in guidance signals execution acceleration. The Iran-related geopolitical headwinds (75bps subscription revenue impact) are temporary [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: ServiceNow is the definitional AI beneficiary in enterprise software — McDermott's thesis that 'AI excels at identifying problems but cannot execute solutions' positions ServiceNow as the execution layer AI agents require [3]. AI revenue target raised 50% to $1.5B in 2026, proving AI is generating incremental demand rather than cannibalization [1]. The company launched AI Governance features ensuring enterprise AI agents operate within ethical and legal boundaries — creating new TAM from AI adoption rather than competing with it [3]. The 22% subscription growth acceleration despite sector-wide AI disruption fears validates the 'AI enabler' positioning conclusively.

infinite_game.summary: Bill McDermott is a rare enterprise software CEO operating with explicitly infinite game thinking — his contract extends to 2030, he purchased $3M in shares during sector fear, and he frames ServiceNow as building infrastructure for a 20+ year enterprise AI transformation. The consistent platform expansion from ITSM to enterprise AI reflects genuine Just Cause commitment.

just_cause.analysis: ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [3]. McDermott's emphasis that AI should amplify humans rather than replace them creates an inclusive, forward-looking vision validated by product outcomes across 8,100+ customers and 80B+ orchestrated workflows annually.

trusting_teams.analysis: ServiceNow consistently ranks on Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For and Glassdoor Best Places to Work with CEO approval above 90% [3]. McDermott's 'human-AI' narrative — framing AI as amplifying employees — creates psychological safety for 29,000+ employees navigating the AI transition. The company's investment in AI governance and human-centered design principles reinforces a trust-first culture.

worthy_rivals.analysis: McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose capabilities push ServiceNow to expand [3]. At Knowledge 2026, he stated ServiceNow 'manages everyone else's board' — framing rivals' AI investments as creating demand for ServiceNow's orchestration layer rather than competitive threats. The Moveworks and Armis acquisitions reflect competitive awareness driving capability expansion.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from ITSM ticketing (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility — each transformation expanded TAM 10x while building on the previous foundation [3]. The company didn't defend its ITSM monopoly; it redefined the category. The agentic AI governance pivot opens yet another transformation vector.

courage_to_lead.analysis: McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sell-off and his public declaration that 'AI intelligence is commoditizing but chaos is coming' — positioning ServiceNow as the governance solution — demonstrate courage to lead through uncertainty [2][3]. Maintaining Rule of 55+ discipline (growth + FCF margin > 55) constrains short-term at the service of long-term platform value.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from — rather than is threatened by — the AI boom. Q1 2026 delivered 22% subscription revenue growth to $3.67B with raised FY2026 guidance to $15.74-15.78B and AI revenue target elevated 50% to $1.5B [1]. Post-10:1 split at $95, the forward P/E of 18.9x and PEG of 0.88 represent the most attractive valuation in years for a consistent 20%+ grower with 32% operating margins. McDermott's $3M personal share purchase, 2030 contract extension, and TIME100-caliber leadership signal maximum conviction in the AI execution layer thesis [2][3].
thesis_continued: The bear case is narrowing but real: the 14-17% stock decline post-Q1 earnings reflected margin compression fears as the company invests aggressively in AI infrastructure and the Armis acquisition adds complexity [1]. Geopolitical headwinds from the Iran conflict delayed several large Middle East deals (75bps subscription impact), and any escalation could pressure international revenue further. The trailing P/E of 56.6x (vs. 18.9x forward) highlights the transition period as GAAP earnings catch up to non-GAAP performance. If agentic AI matures to replace workflow orchestration rather than require it, the entire thesis inverts.
key_catalysts: AI revenue accelerating toward $1.5B target in 2026 — 50% above original guidance — validating enterprise AI agent adoption at scale
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, expanding platform TAM to security operations and adding ~1% incremental revenue
Forward P/E compression to 18.9x with PEG of 0.88 creating compelling entry point for a consistent 20%+ subscription revenue grower
key_risks: Agentic AI systems maturing to replace enterprise workflow orchestration within 2-3 years, inverting the 'execution layer' thesis
Middle East geopolitical escalation extending deal delays beyond the 75bps Q1 impact into material full-year subscription growth headwinds
Margin compression from aggressive AI investment and Armis integration costs disappointing investors who expect expanding profitability
